title: Physical page topology
description: JSON Schema for physical.json — defines the physical network topology including nodes (devices), edges (cable connections), hardware specifications, and switch port mappings. This schema validates data used by the Physical page view in the Lab Map application. Each node represents a physical device (router, switch, server, etc.) with detailed hardware specs, while edges represent actual cable runs between ports.

nodes.$comment: Array of physical devices in the network topology
nodes.description: List of all physical nodes (devices) in the network. Each node has a unique ID, display label, device type, and metadata containing hardware specifications.

edges.$comment: Array of physical cable connections between devices
edges.description: List of all physical connections (cables) between nodes. Each edge specifies source/target ports, cable type, speed, and VLAN assignments.

macAddress.$comment: MAC address format validation - lowercase hex with hyphens
macAddress.description: Network interface MAC address in lowercase hexadecimal with hyphens (e.g., 48-0f-cf-47-68-df). Use 'unknown' if MAC is not known.

nic.$comment: Network Interface Card definition - port details for hardware specs
nic.description: Network interface card configuration including port name, link speed, MAC address, and what it connects to.

name.description: Interface name as shown in OS (e.g., eth0, enp3s0, lan1)

connected_to.description: What device/port this NIC connects to (e.g., core-sw:port5)

storageDevice.$comment: Storage device definition - drives attached to a node
storageDevice.description: Storage device specification including device path, capacity, type, model, and performance characteristics.

hardware.$comment: Hardware specifications block - detailed device specs
hardware.description: Complete hardware specification for a node including CPU, RAM, network interfaces, and storage devices.

model.description: Device model name/number (e.g., Dell PowerEdge R720, Raspberry Pi 4B)

cpu.description: CPU model (e.g., Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2, AMD Ryzen 5 5600G)

wol.description: Wake-on-LAN capability (true if supported and enabled)

nodeMeta.$comment: Node metadata - hostname, role, network info, and hardware specs
nodeMeta.description: Metadata for a physical node containing identification info (hostname, role) and optional hardware specifications.

role.description: Brief role description (e.g., Core Router, Primary NAS, K3s Control Plane)

node.$comment: Physical node definition - a device in the network topology
node.description: A physical network node representing a device such as a router, switch, access point, or server.

id.description: Unique identifier for this node (used in edge from/to references)

label.description: Display label shown on the graph

type.description: Node type determining visual style and icon. Must match a folder name in public/node-icons/.

meta.description: Node metadata with hostname, role, and hardware specs

edge.$comment: Physical edge definition - a cable connection between nodes
edge.description: A physical connection (cable) between two nodes, specifying ports, speed, and VLAN configuration.

from.description: Source endpoint with required nodeId and optional port

to.description: Target endpoint with required nodeId and optional port

type.description: Connection type: wan (ISP uplink), trunk (carries multiple VLANs), access (single VLAN)

label.description: Display label for this edge (can be empty string)

vlans.description: VLAN IDs carried by this link (e.g., 10,20,30 or 10-50)

cable.description: Cable type (e.g., Cat6, Cat6a, OM4 fiber)

category.description: Cable category for Ethernet (e.g., Cat5e, Cat6, Cat6a)

arrows.description: Arrow direction for vis-network (e.g., to, from, to;from)

edgeEndpoint.description: Edge endpoint containing node reference and optional port identifier.
